hypothesis: Under DAC-relevant conditions (298 K, 0.4 mbar CO2, 40% RH), polyamines engineered to present a local hydrophobic environment (≥0.6 fraction of aliphatic carbons within 0.5 nm of each secondary amine) will exhibit ≥25% higher equilibrium CO2 uptake than compositionally matched linear hydrophilic analogs with identical amine density, due to reduced competitive amine–H2O binding.
